action.hypothesis: Round 41 made the optimizer question clear: `gated+adam` is not merely slightly worse under the updated anchor, it shows the same pathological curve shape as before, with a strong mid-training blow-up and a much worse endpoint than `signum`. With the optimizer branch now effectively closed, the next single question in the prescribed order is loss: under the strengthened `gated` mainline recipe at `K=128, EF=12, signum, LR=2.0e-3`, is the default auxiliary loss actually helping, or does removing it improve the full training trajectory?
action.summary: Stay on the active `gated` family and return to the `signum` anchor at `ARCHITECTURE=gated`, `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `LR=2.0e-3`, with the same runtime knobs pinned, and change only the loss-side variable `AUXK_ALPHA` from `0.03125` to `0.0`. Judge this run by curve shape as well as terminal FVU: if dropping aux yields a smoother or lower full trajectory than the current `0.148156` anchor, then the stronger LR has made the auxiliary term unnecessary or harmful; if it regresses or destabilizes training, keep the default aux loss and move on to preprocess.
action.self_review: This keeps the search on the same family, respects the fixed `EF=12` rule, and changes exactly one non-core variable after the optimizer branch was cleanly falsified twice. I am not spending another round on Adam LR because the updated `adam` trial was not borderline or merely mistuned; it repeated the same qualitative failure pattern and got worse at the stronger anchor. The main risk is that aux removal may have only a small effect, but it is now the next most policy-clean question before preprocess or K movement.
notes_to_memory: Round 41 strongly rejected `adam` for `gated` again: compared with the `signum` anchor it showed the same mid-curve blow-up pattern as the earlier `adam` trial and finished far worse, so the optimizer branch should be treated as closed.

Round 42 returns to the updated `gated+signum` anchor at `LR=2.0e-3` and isolates the loss axis by setting `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` to test whether the default auxiliary term is still helping under the stronger mainline recipe.
next_hypotheses: If `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` improves the curve or final FVU, keep loss-side tuning alive with at most one nearby confirmation on the same anchor.
If removing aux is worse or neutral, restore the default loss setting and move to the preprocess axis before any further K exploration.

result.description: Stay on the active `gated` family and return to the `signum` anchor at `ARCHITECTURE=gated`, `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `LR=2.0e-3`, with the same runtime knobs pinned, and change only the loss-side variable `AUXK_ALPHA` from `0.03125` to `0.0`. Judge this run by curve shape as well as terminal FVU: if dropping aux yields a smoother or lower full trajectory than the current `0.148156` anchor, then the stronger LR has made the auxiliary term unnecessary or harmful; if it regresses or destabilizes training, keep the default aux loss and move on to preprocess.

I am not spending another round on Adam LR because the updated `adam` trial was not borderline or merely mistuned; it repeated the same qualitative failure pattern and got worse at the stronger anchor. The main risk is that aux removal may have only a small effect, but it is now the next most policy-clean question before preprocess or K movement.
